content.full_text:
Fetn Poltrrcer PRACTTcEs LouurssroN
428 J Street r Suite 620 r Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
March 23,2006
Jennifer Rindahl, Associate
The Lew Edwards Group
P.O. Box 21215
Oakland, CA 94620
RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-06-043
Dear Ms. Rindahl:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the campaign
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").r It is dated Thursday, tvtarch t6, Z0OA
and appears to have been faxed to our offices just before five o'clock that day.
The purpose of this letter is to confirm the substance of what I told you in our
telephone conversation the morning of Monday, March 20,2006. UnfortunatelS we
cannot provide you with written advice on such a short timeline. Pursuant to section
831 14(b), the Commission provides written advice within 21 working days of the request,
provided that the time may be extended for good cause. In addition, that time period does
not commence until all the requirements of regulation 18329(b)(2) are satisfied. (See
copy of regulation 18329, attached hereto.) Although we sometimes can provide advice
upon an expedited basis, depending upon agency resources, it is rarely possible to
provide written legal advice within two working days.
I Government Code sections 81000 - g1}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations. A11 further references to "sections" and "regulations,,
are to the Government Code and Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations, respectively, unless
otherwise indicated.


File No. W-06-043
Page No. 2
In our March 20 conversation, I also informed you that you are not precluded
from seeking advice from privatelyhired counsel.
If you have any questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By: Andreas C. Rockas
Counsel, Legal Division

sections.question_synthetic: The requestor sought expedited written advice on campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act on a very short timeline (two working days).
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC cannot provide written advice within the requested two-working-day timeline, as the Commission provides written advice within 21 working days of request pursuant to section 83114(b), and the requestor is directed to seek privately-hired counsel if needed.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a procedural response letter confirming prior telephone advice rather than a substantive advice letter with detailed Q/A sections. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present in the document structure. The substance of the inquiry is not detailed, only the procedural limitation is addressed.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requestor sought expedited written advice on campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act on a very short timeline (two working days).

CONCLUSION: The FPPC cannot provide written advice within the requested two-working-day timeline, as the Commission provides written advice within 21 working days of request pursuant to section 83114(b), and the requestor is directed to seek privately-hired counsel if needed.

embedding.summary: The FPPC confirms telephonic advice given to the requestor and explains that it cannot provide written legal advice within the requested two-day timeline due to its standard 21 working day response period under section 83114(b).
